citation.sourceDocument: Missouri DOR individual income tax rate schedule (top 4.7%, SB 3 2022); federal-conformity standard deduction
citation.sourceNote: Missouri's eight-tier schedule is identical for every filing status (RSMo 143.011): 0% on the first $1,313 of taxable income, then 2% / 2.5% / 3% / 3.5% / 4% / 4.5% in $1,313 increments, and a top rate of 4.7% above $9,191 — the rate reduced from 4.8% to 4.7% effective 2025 under the SB 3 (2022) revenue triggers and stays 4.7% for 2026. These are the MO DOR's latest officially published thresholds (its 2025 'year changes' schedule); the 2026 CPI-indexed brackets shift each tier up by about $35 (the top reaches $9,436 per the Tax Foundation 2026 table), immaterial to the computed tax since all income above ~$9,200 is taxed at 4.7% either way, and they roll as the reviewer's data-only step. The standard deduction equals the federal standard deduction by statute, shown here at the confirmed 2026 federal amounts ($16,100 / $32,200 / $24,150). Missouri has no personal or dependent exemption (TCJA conformity zeroed it). The Missouri deduction for a percentage of federal income tax paid (phased down from 35% to 0% of federal tax as Missouri AGI rises, capped at $5,000 / $10,000) is omitted at launch fidelity — worth roughly $15-50 of Missouri tax for a typical filer — so the figure errs slightly high, the conservative side. Kansas City and St. Louis local earnings taxes (1%) are out of scope like other municipal add-ons. Cross-checked against the Tax Foundation 2026 table; verified on 2026-06-06.
